assumption_note: Individually-quick-frozen ramen noodle block -- ~2.5 oz each, verified: "NOODLE RAMEN IQF COOKED SUN 80CT" implies $4.15/lb, plausible for ramen noodles.
Whole red seeded watermelon -- ~20 lb each (a low count-per-case, e.g. "3ct", confirms these are whole melons not cut pieces). Verified: implies $0.41/lb, plausible.
Standard frozen appetizer samosa -- ~1 oz each. Verified: implies $5.06/lb, plausible for a frozen appetizer.
"CT" here means individual snack bags per case, not pods -- a standard edamame snack bag runs ~3 oz. Verified: implies $3.51/lb, plausible.

Small (2-2.13 in) dessert tart shell -- mostly hollow pastry, ~0.4 oz each. Lowest confidence in this file -- implies a relatively high $10-20/lb, but tart shells are a low-weight specialty pastry component, not a bulk commodity, so a higher $/lb is plausible even if the exact oz figure is uncertain.
Petite/mini corn cob -- much smaller than a full ear, ~2.5 oz each (same proxy as the UCB/UCSC "cobette" entries).
Standard slicer cucumber -- ~0.66 lb (300g) each, same proxy as UCB/UCSC.
Standard mango -- ~0.75 lb each, same proxy as UCB/UCSC.

Standard Hass avocado case -- ~25 lb net regardless of the count/size grade in the name, same standard-carton reasoning as the UCB/UCSC avocado entries.

Standard US apple shipping carton -- 40 lb net regardless of the count/size grade in the name (e.g. "88/90CT"), same standard-carton reasoning as the UCB/UCSC apple entries. Sidesteps needing to parse a size-grade range as a count at all.
Standard Western pear shipping box -- ~44 lb net regardless of the count/size grade in the name (e.g. "90/100 CT"), same standard-carton reasoning as the UCB/UCSC pear entries.

Standard deli bagel -- ~4.5 oz each, the same figure this export's own "BAGEL BLUEBERRY 4.5oz BAKEMARK"/"BAGEL PLAIN 4.5oz BAKEMARK" SKUs state explicitly (a real stated weight elsewhere in this file, not a blind guess). Verified across 8 bare-EACH bagel rows with no oz of their own: implies $1.35-3.38/lb, all plausible.
Standard garlic cheese breadstick -- ~1.5 oz each. Verified: "BREADSTICKS GARLIC CHS 8"" (125/CS) implies $3.65/lb, plausible.

Standard sliced sourdough loaf -- ~1.25 lb each. Verified: (10/CS) implies $3.06/lb, plausible. Deliberately anchored to this exact SKU rather than a bare "SOURDOUGH" keyword -- "AVB BIG SOURDOUGH PANOR" is a different (larger, "BIG") product and shouldn't reuse this same reference weight.

Thin wafer "cake cone" for ice cream service (not a dessert cake) -- ~0.15 oz each, very light. Verified: "CONE CAKE #10 36/20CT" (a self-contained 36x20=720 chain, Purchase Unit redundant with it) implies ~$0.073/cone and $7.78/lb, both plausible for bulk foodservice wafer cones.

23"x11" par-baked sheet pizza crust, sold "20PC" per case (Purchase Unit "20/CS") -- no per-piece weight stated anywhere, so this is a full-case estimate (~2.75 lb/piece x 20 = 55 lb/case) rather than a per-piece figure. Verified: implies $2.27/lb, plausible for wholesale par-baked crust. Lowest-confidence entry in this file -- flagged for review since the per-piece weight is a blind estimate with no stated anchor at all.

Standard cake/yeast donut, assorted -- ~2.5 oz each. Purchase Unit "DOZ" is handled via the "DOZ"->12 mapping folded into the bare-count fallback (pu_bare_ct), same mechanism as bare "NCT". Verified: "ASSORTED DONUT" (1,890 dozen) implies $6.81/lb, plausible.

Sibling SKU "SW BREAD SUB ROLL/WHOLE WHEAT 80CT" (same file) states Purchase Unit "CS80" -- 80 rolls/case, a real cross-referenced count, not a guess. At ~2.5 oz/roll that's a 12.5 lb case. Verified: implies $2.12/lb, plausible. Anchored to the exact "/WHITE" SKU (bare Purchase Unit "CASE", no count of its own) rather than the generic "SW BREAD SUB ROLL" pattern below, which the 80CT sibling already matches via its own name-stated count.
Standard sub sandwich roll -- ~2.5 oz each, same figure as the case-formula entry above. Verified against the 80CT sibling (which states its own count in the name): implies $2.15/lb, plausible.

Sibling SKUs "HOT POCKET MEATBALL W/ MOZZ" and "HOT POCKET HAM & CHEESE 8OZ" (same file) both use Purchase Unit "12/CS" -- a real cross-referenced case count of 12 for this product line, applied here since this specific SKU's own Purchase Unit is bare "CASE" (no count). At the stated 8 oz/box that's a 6 lb case. Verified: implies $3.66/lb, matching the sibling's $3.66/lb exactly.
Standard 8-inch personal pizza -- ~10 oz each. Verified: (24/CS) implies $3.40/lb, plausible.
12-inch wood-fired-style thin pizza crust -- ~7 oz each. Purchase Unit "CS24" (24 per case) picked up via the "CSN" case-count fallback. Verified: implies $4.68/lb, plausible.

"Balloon Parisian" style French bread loaf -- ~18 oz each. Purchase Unit "LOAF" (one loaf per unit, same convention as "EACH") picked up via the "LOAF"->1 fallback. Verified: implies $5.78/lb, plausible.
Standard sliced sandwich-style Texas toast loaf -- ~20 oz each. Purchase Unit "LOAF" picked up via the "LOAF"->1 fallback. Verified: implies $3.27/lb, plausible.

"ASST/48" (48 assorted mini danishes per tray) isn't parsed as a count by the shape extractor ("ASST" before the slash isn't numeric) -- treated as a full-tray weight instead: 48 x ~1.5 oz/danish = 4.5 lb/tray. Verified: implies $8.75/lb, plausible.

Generic bakery croissant (no explicit oz stated) -- ~3 oz each, verified across 4 Starbucks croissant SKUs (Ham & Cheese, Chocolate, Butter, Baked Apple) all sold bare "EACH": implies $5.06-9.76/lb, all plausible and internally consistent.

Generic bakery danish (no explicit oz stated) -- ~2 oz each, verified across 4 SKUs (Cheese 'N Berry, Apple, Starbucks Cheese Danish, LE CHEF via the tray=6 fallback) all sold bare "EACH"/"TRAY": implies $7.60-13.12/lb, all plausible and internally consistent. Deliberately does not affect "DOUGH DANISH...OZ"/"...IW 12/3OZ" sibling SKUs -- those already resolve earlier via their own explicit weight chains before reaching this dictionary.
Small (5-inch) individual deep-dish pie shell -- ~2.5 oz each (mostly hollow pastry). Verified: (84/CS) implies $4.52/lb, plausible.

Specialty multigrain flatbread, ~8.5x9.5in -- ~10 oz each, scaled against the existing Sysco lavash flatbread proxy (11x17in, 8oz) but denser/thicker. Lower confidence than most entries here -- no stated anchor at all, verified only that it lands under the $20/lb ceiling ($14.31/lb).
"Big Bopper"-style novelty ice cream cookie sandwich -- ~4.5 oz each. Verified: (12/CS) implies $5.69/lb, plausible.
Korean tteokbokki (topokki) rice cake, frozen bag -- ~1 lb each. Verified: (16/CS) implies $2.94/lb, plausible.

Standard (non-mini) brioche bun -- ~2 oz each. Checked after UCB's "BUN BRIOCHE.*MINI" entry (sysco_ct_food_dictionary), which takes priority for mini SKUs. Verified across 2 TURANO SKUs: "BUN BRIOCHE 4.5" TURANO 10CT" (bare count 10 x Purchase Unit "8/CS" multiplier) implies $4.80/lb; "BUN BRIOCHE 4" TURANO 4/24CT" (self-contained name chain 4x24=96) implies $4.08/lb -- both plausible and consistent.

"64oz" read as the stated total case weight (4 lb) for this cut 12x16 cornbread pan, rather than guessed. Verified: implies $12.62/lb -- higher than typical bulk cornbread, so this is a lower-confidence entry (the "12x16" pan size may suggest a heavier true weight than 64oz alone implies), but no better anchor exists in this file.

No sibling within UCR itself, but UCLA_H's own export (a different distributor, US Foods, not Sysco) has an almost identically-described product -- "BREAD, WHEAT GARLIC SLICED 1 LOAF PARBAKED FROZEN BULK TEXAS TOAST" (Hilltop Hearth brand) -- with a DIRECTLY reported weight: 21.0 lb / 2 units = 10.5 lb/case. Cross-distributor, not same-SKU, so lower confidence than the Sysco cross-campus lookup elsewhere in this project, but real stated data rather than a blind guess. Verified: implies $3.63/lb, plausible for garlic bread.
